   426→// handleLifecycleSubagentEnd handles subagent end: detects changes, saves task checkpoint.
   427→func handleLifecycleSubagentEnd(ag agent.Agent, event *agent.Event) error {
   428→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "lifecycle"), ag.Name())
   429→	logging.Info(logCtx, "subagent-end",
   430→		slog.String("event", event.Type.String()),
   431→		slog.String("session_id", event.SessionID),
   432→		slog.String("tool_use_id", event.ToolUseID),
   433→		slog.String("subagent_id", event.SubagentID),
   434→	)
   435→
   436→	// Extract subagent type and description from tool input
   437→	subagentType, taskDescription := ParseSubagentTypeAndDescription(event.ToolInput)
   438→
   439→	// Determine subagent transcript path
   440→	transcriptDir := filepath.Dir(event.SessionRef)
   441→	var subagentTranscriptPath string
   442→	if event.SubagentID != "" {
   443→		subagentTranscriptPath = AgentTranscriptPath(transcriptDir, event.SubagentID)
   444→		if !fileExists(subagentTranscriptPath) {
   445→			subagentTranscriptPath = ""
   446→		}
   447→	}
   448→
   449→	// Log context
   450→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "[entire] Subagent completed\n")
   451→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "  Session ID: %s\n", event.SessionID)
   452→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "  Tool Use ID: %s\n", event.ToolUseID)
   453→	if event.SubagentID != "" {
   454→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "  Agent ID: %s\n", event.SubagentID)
   455→	}
   456→	if subagentTranscriptPath != "" {
   457→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "  Subagent Transcript: %s\n", subagentTranscriptPath)
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	// Extract modified files from subagent transcript
   461→	var modifiedFiles []string
   462→	if analyzer, ok := ag.(agent.TranscriptAnalyzer); ok {
   463→		transcriptToScan := event.SessionRef
   464→		if subagentTranscriptPath != "" {
   465→			transcriptToScan = subagentTranscriptPath
   466→		}
   467→		if files, _, fileErr := analyzer.ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset(transcriptToScan, 0); fileErr != nil {
   468→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract modified files from subagent: %v\n", fileErr)
   469→		} else {
   470→			modifiedFiles = files
   471→		}
   472→	}
   473→
   474→	// Load pre-task state and detect file changes
   475→	preState, err := LoadPreTaskState(event.ToolUseID)
   476→	if err != nil {
   477→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-task state: %v\n", err)
   478→	}
   479→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   480→	if err != nil {
   481→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   482→	}
   483→
   484→	// Get repo root and normalize paths
   485→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   486→	if err != nil {
   487→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   488→	}
   489→
   490→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   491→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   492→	if changes != nil {
   493→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   494→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   495→	}
   496→
   497→	// If no changes, skip
   498→	if len(relModifiedFiles) == 0 && len(relNewFiles) == 0 && len(relDeletedFiles) == 0 {
   499→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping task checkpoint\n")
   500→		_ = CleanupPreTaskState(event.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   501→		return nil
   502→	}
   503→
   504→	// Find checkpoint UUID from main transcript (best-effort)
   505→	var checkpointUUID string
   506→	if analyzer, ok := ag.(agent.TranscriptAnalyzer); ok {
   507→		// Use ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset on the main transcript to get position info
   508→		// and then look for checkpoint UUID via the legacy path
   509→		_ = analyzer // UUID lookup requires transcript parsing — delegate to existing helper
   510→	}
   511→	// Fall back to the existing CLI-level checkpoint UUID finder
   512→	mainLines, _, _ := parseTranscriptForCheckpointUUID(event.SessionRef) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort
   513→	if mainLines != nil {
   514→		checkpointUUID, _ = FindCheckpointUUID(mainLines, event.ToolUseID)
   515→	}
   516→
   517→	// Get git author
   518→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   519→	if err != nil {
   520→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	// Build task checkpoint context
   524→	strat := GetStrategy()
   525→	agentType := ag.Type()
   526→
   527→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   528→		SessionID:              event.SessionID,
   529→		ToolUseID:              event.ToolUseID,
   530→		AgentID:                event.SubagentID,
   531→		ModifiedFiles:          relModifiedFiles,
   532→		NewFiles:               relNewFiles,
   533→		DeletedFiles:           relDeletedFiles,
   534→		TranscriptPath:         event.SessionRef,
   535→		SubagentTranscriptPath: subagentTranscriptPath,
   536→		CheckpointUUID:         checkpointUUID,
   537→		AuthorName:             author.Name,
   538→		AuthorEmail:            author.Email,
   539→		SubagentType:           subagentType,
   540→		TaskDescription:        taskDescription,
   541→		AgentType:              agentType,
   542→	}
   543→
   544→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   545→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save task checkpoint: %w", err)
   546→	}
   547→
   548→	_ = CleanupPreTaskState(event.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   549→	return nil
   550→}

   553→
   554→// resolveTranscriptOffset determines the transcript offset to use for parsing.
   555→// Prefers pre-prompt state, falls back to session state.
   556→func resolveTranscriptOffset(preState *PrePromptState, sessionID string) int {
   557→	if preState != nil && preState.TranscriptOffset > 0 {
   558→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state found: parsing transcript from offset %d\n", preState.TranscriptOffset)
   559→		return preState.TranscriptOffset
   560→	}
   561→
   562→	// Fall back to session state (e.g., auto-commit strategy updates it after each save)
   563→	sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   564→	if loadErr != nil {
   565→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   566→		return 0
   567→	}
   568→	if sessionState != nil && sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart > 0 {
   569→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session state found: parsing transcript from offset %d\n", sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   570→		return sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart
   571→	}
   572→
   573→	return 0
   574→}
   575→
   576→// updateAutoCommitTranscriptPosition updates the session state with the new transcript position
   577→// for the auto-commit strategy.
   578→func updateAutoCommitTranscriptPosition(sessionID string, newPosition int) {
   579→	sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   580→	if loadErr != nil {
   581→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   582→		return
   583→	}
   584→	if sessionState == nil {
   585→		sessionState = &strategy.SessionState{
   586→			SessionID: sessionID,
   587→		}
   588→	}
   589→	sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart = newPosition
   590→	sessionState.StepCount++
   591→	if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(sessionState); updateErr != nil {
   592→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session state: %v\n", updateErr)
   593→	} else {
   594→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Updated session state: transcript position=%d, checkpoint=%d\n",
   595→			newPosition, sessionState.StepCount)
   596→	}
   597→}

   599→// createContextFile creates a context.md file for the session checkpoint.
   600→// This is a unified version that works for all agents.
   601→func createContextFile(contextFile, commitMessage, sessionID string, prompts []string, summary string) error {
   602→	var sb strings.Builder
   603→
   604→	sb.WriteString("# Session Context\n\n")
   605→	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Session ID: %s\n", sessionID))
   606→	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Commit Message: %s\n\n", commitMessage))
   607→
   608→	if len(prompts) > 0 {
   609→		sb.WriteString("## Prompts\n\n")
   610→		for i, p := range prompts {
   611→			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("### Prompt %d\n\n%s\n\n", i+1, p))
   612→		}
   613→	}
   614→
   615→	if summary != "" {
   616→		sb.WriteString("## Summary\n\n")
   617→		sb.WriteString(summary)
   618→		sb.WriteString("\n")
   619→	}
   620→
   621→	if err := os.WriteFile(contextFile, []byte(sb.String()), 0o600); err != nil {
   622→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write context file: %w", err)
   623→	}
   624→	return nil
   625→}
   626→
   627→// parseTranscriptForCheckpointUUID is a thin wrapper around transcript parsing for checkpoint UUID lookup.
   628→// Returns parsed transcript lines for use with FindCheckpointUUID.
   629→func parseTranscriptForCheckpointUUID(transcriptPath string) ([]transcriptLine, int, error) {
   630→	lines, total, err := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, 0)
   631→	if err != nil {
   632→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("parsing transcript for checkpoint UUID: %w", err)
   633→	}
   634→	return lines, total, nil
   635→}

    20→// PrePromptState stores the state captured before a user prompt
    21→type PrePromptState struct {
    22→	SessionID      string   `json:"session_id"`
    23→	Timestamp      string   `json:"timestamp"`
    24→	UntrackedFiles []string `json:"untracked_files"`
    25→
    26→	// TranscriptOffset is the unified transcript position when this state was captured.
    27→	// For Claude Code (JSONL), this is the line count.
    28→	// For Gemini CLI (JSON), this is the message count.
    29→	// Zero means not set or session just started.
    30→	TranscriptOffset int `json:"transcript_offset,omitempty"`
    31→
    32→	// LastTranscriptIdentifier is the agent-specific identifier at the transcript position.
    33→	// UUID for Claude Code, message ID for Gemini CLI. Optional metadata.
    34→	LastTranscriptIdentifier string `json:"last_transcript_identifier,omitempty"`
    35→
    36→	// Deprecated: StartMessageIndex is the old Gemini-specific field.
    37→	// Migrated to TranscriptOffset on load.
    38→	StartMessageIndex int `json:"start_message_index,omitempty"`
    39→
    40→	// Deprecated: StepTranscriptStart is the old Claude-specific field.
    41→	// Migrated to TranscriptOffset on load.
    42→	StepTranscriptStart int `json:"step_transcript_start,omitempty"`
    43→
    44→	// Deprecated: LastTranscriptLineCount is the oldest name for transcript position.
    45→	// Migrated to TranscriptOffset on load.
    46→	LastTranscriptLineCount int `json:"last_transcript_line_count,omitempty"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// PreUntrackedFiles returns the untracked files list, or nil if the receiver is nil.
    50→// This nil-vs-empty distinction lets DetectFileChanges know whether to skip new-file detection.
    51→// When the receiver is non-nil but UntrackedFiles is nil (e.g., old state files deserialized with
    52→// "untracked_files": null), returns an empty non-nil slice so that all current untracked files
    53→// are correctly treated as new.
    54→func (s *PrePromptState) PreUntrackedFiles() []string {
    55→	if s == nil {
    56→		return nil
    57→	}
    58→	if s.UntrackedFiles == nil {
    59→		return []string{}
    60→	}
    61→	return s.UntrackedFiles
    62→}
    63→
    64→// normalizePrePromptState migrates deprecated fields after loading from JSON.
    65→func (s *PrePromptState) normalizePrePromptState() {
    66→	// Migrate the oldest field to the intermediate field first
    67→	if s.StepTranscriptStart == 0 && s.LastTranscriptLineCount > 0 {
    68→		s.StepTranscriptStart = s.LastTranscriptLineCount
    69→	}
    70→	s.LastTranscriptLineCount = 0
    71→
    72→	// Migrate all deprecated fields to the unified TranscriptOffset
    73→	if s.TranscriptOffset == 0 {
    74→		if s.StepTranscriptStart > 0 {
    75→			s.TranscriptOffset = s.StepTranscriptStart
    76→		} else if s.StartMessageIndex > 0 {
    77→			s.TranscriptOffset = s.StartMessageIndex
    78→		}
    79→	}
    80→	s.StepTranscriptStart = 0
    81→	s.StartMessageIndex = 0
    82→}
    83→
    84→// CapturePrePromptState captures current untracked files and transcript position before a prompt
    85→// and saves them to a state file.
    86→//
    87→// The agent parameter is used to determine the transcript position via TranscriptAnalyzer.
    88→// If the agent does not implement TranscriptAnalyzer, the transcript offset will be 0.
    89→// The sessionRef parameter is optional — if empty, transcript position won't be captured.
    90→//
    91→// Works correctly from any subdirectory within the repository.
    92→func CapturePrePromptState(ag agent.Agent, sessionID, sessionRef string) error {
    93→	if sessionID == "" {
    94→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	// Get absolute path for tmp directory
    98→	tmpDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireTmpDir)
    99→	if err != nil {
   100→		tmpDirAbs = paths.EntireTmpDir // Fallback to relative
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	// Create tmp directory if it doesn't exist
   104→	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDirAbs, 0o750); err != nil {
   105→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create tmp directory: %w", err)
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Get list of untracked files (excluding .entire directory itself)
   109→	untrackedFiles, err := getUntrackedFilesForState()
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get untracked files: %w", err)
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// Get transcript position using TranscriptAnalyzer if available
   115→	var transcriptOffset int
   116→	if analyzer, ok := ag.(agent.TranscriptAnalyzer); ok && sessionRef != "" {
   117→		pos, posErr := analyzer.GetTranscriptPosition(sessionRef)
   118→		if posErr != nil {
   119→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get transcript position: %v\n", posErr)
   120→		} else {
   121→			transcriptOffset = pos
   122→		}
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	// Create state file
   126→	stateFile := prePromptStateFile(sessionID)
   127→	state := PrePromptState{
   128→		SessionID:        sessionID,
   129→		Timestamp:        time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   130→		UntrackedFiles:   untrackedFiles,
   131→		TranscriptOffset: transcriptOffset,
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	data, err := jsonutil.MarshalIndentWithNewline(state, "", "  ")
   135→	if err != nil {
   136→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal state: %w", err)
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	if err := os.WriteFile(stateFile, data, 0o600); err != nil {
   140→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write state file: %w", err)
   141→	}
   142→
   143→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Captured state before prompt: %d untracked files, transcript offset: %d\n",
   144→		len(untrackedFiles), transcriptOffset)
   145→	return nil
   146→}

   181→// FileChanges holds categorized file changes from git status.
   182→type FileChanges struct {
   183→	Modified []string // Modified or staged files
   184→	New      []string // Untracked files (filtered if previouslyUntracked provided)
   185→	Deleted  []string // Deleted files (staged or unstaged)
   186→}
   187→
   188→// DetectFileChanges returns categorized file changes from the current git status.
   189→//
   190→// previouslyUntracked controls new-file detection:
   191→//   - nil: all untracked files go into New
   192→//   - non-nil: only untracked files NOT in the pre-existing set go into New
   193→//
   194→// Modified includes both worktree and staging modified/added files.
   195→// Deleted includes both staged and unstaged deletions.
   196→// All results exclude .entire/ directory.
   197→func DetectFileChanges(previouslyUntracked []string) (*FileChanges, error) {
   198→	repo, err := openRepository()
   199→	if err != nil {
   200→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open repository: %w", err)
   201→	}
   202→
   203→	worktree, err := repo.Worktree()
   204→	if err != nil {
   205→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get worktree: %w", err)
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	status, err := worktree.Status()
   209→	if err != nil {
   210→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get status: %w", err)
   211→	}
   212→
   213→	// Build set of pre-existing untracked files for quick lookup
   214→	var preExisting map[string]bool
   215→	if previouslyUntracked != nil {
   216→		preExisting = make(map[string]bool, len(previouslyUntracked))
   217→		for _, f := range previouslyUntracked {
   218→			preExisting[f] = true
   219→		}
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	var changes FileChanges
   223→	for file, st := range status {
   224→		if paths.IsInfrastructurePath(file) {
   225→			continue
   226→		}
   227→
   228→		switch {
   229→		case st.Worktree == git.Untracked:
   230→			if preExisting != nil {
   231→				if !preExisting[file] {
   232→					changes.New = append(changes.New, file)
   233→				}
   234→			} else {
   235→				changes.New = append(changes.New, file)
   236→			}
   237→		case st.Worktree == git.Deleted || st.Staging == git.Deleted:
   238→			changes.Deleted = append(changes.Deleted, file)
   239→		case st.Worktree == git.Modified || st.Staging == git.Modified ||
   240→			st.Worktree == git.Added || st.Staging == git.Added:
   241→			changes.Modified = append(changes.Modified, file)
   242→		}
   243→	}
   244→
   245→	return &changes, nil
   246→}

   248→// FilterAndNormalizePaths converts absolute paths to relative and filters out
   249→// infrastructure paths and paths outside the repo.
   250→func FilterAndNormalizePaths(files []string, cwd string) []string {
   251→	var result []string
   252→	for _, file := range files {
   253→		relPath := paths.ToRelativePath(file, cwd)
   254→		if relPath == "" {
   255→			continue // outside repo
   256→		}
   257→		if paths.IsInfrastructurePath(relPath) {
   258→			continue // skip .entire directory
   259→		}
   260→		result = append(result, relPath)
   261→	}
   262→	return result
   263→}

   275→// getUntrackedFilesForState returns a list of untracked files using go-git

   277→func getUntrackedFilesForState() ([]string, error) {
   278→	repo, err := openRepository()
   279→	if err != nil {

   281→	}
   282→
   283→	worktree, err := repo.Worktree()
   284→	if err != nil {

   287→
   288→	status, err := worktree.Status()
   289→	if err != nil {
   290→		return nil, err //nolint:wrapcheck // already present in codebase
   291→	}
   292→
   293→	untrackedFiles := []string{}
   294→	for file, st := range status {
   295→		if st.Worktree == git.Untracked {

   297→			if !strings.HasPrefix(file, paths.EntireDir+"/") && file != paths.EntireDir {

   325→// CapturePreTaskState captures current untracked files before a Task execution
   326→// and saves them to a state file.
   327→// Works correctly from any subdirectory within the repository.
   328→func CapturePreTaskState(toolUseID string) error {
   329→	if toolUseID == "" {
   330→		return errors.New("tool_use_id is required")
   331→	}
   332→
   333→	// Get absolute path for tmp directory
   334→	tmpDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireTmpDir)
   335→	if err != nil {
   336→		tmpDirAbs = paths.EntireTmpDir // Fallback to relative
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	// Create tmp directory if it doesn't exist
   340→	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDirAbs, 0o750); err != nil {
   341→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create tmp directory: %w", err)
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	// Get list of untracked files (excluding .entire directory itself)
   345→	untrackedFiles, err := getUntrackedFilesForState()
   346→	if err != nil {
   347→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get untracked files: %w", err)
   348→	}
   349→
   350→	// Create state file
   351→	stateFile := preTaskStateFile(toolUseID)
   352→	state := PreTaskState{
   353→		ToolUseID:      toolUseID,
   354→		Timestamp:      time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   355→		UntrackedFiles: untrackedFiles,
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	data, err := jsonutil.MarshalIndentWithNewline(state, "", "  ")
   359→	if err != nil {
   360→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal state: %w", err)
   361→	}
   362→
   363→	if err := os.WriteFile(stateFile, data, 0o600); err != nil {
   364→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write state file: %w", err)
   365→	}
   366→
   367→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Captured state before task: %d untracked files\n", len(untrackedFiles))
   368→	return nil
   369→}

   411→
   412→// preTaskFilePrefix is the prefix for pre-task state files
   413→const preTaskFilePrefix = "pre-task-"
   414→
   415→// FindActivePreTaskFile finds an active pre-task file in .entire/tmp/ and returns
   416→// the parent Task's tool_use_id. Returns ("", false) if no pre-task file exists.
   417→// When multiple pre-task files exist (nested subagents), returns the most recently
   418→// modified one.
   419→// Works correctly from any subdirectory within the repository.
   420→func FindActivePreTaskFile() (taskToolUseID string, found bool) {
   421→	tmpDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireTmpDir)
   422→	if err != nil {
   423→		tmpDirAbs = paths.EntireTmpDir // Fallback to relative
   424→	}
   425→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDirAbs)
   426→	if err != nil {
   427→		return "", false
   428→	}
   429→
   430→	var latestFile string
   431→	var latestTime time.Time
   432→
   433→	for _, entry := range entries {
   434→		if entry.IsDir() {
   435→			continue
   436→		}
   437→		name := entry.Name()
   438→		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, preTaskFilePrefix) || !strings.HasSuffix(name, ".json") {
   439→			continue
   440→		}
   441→
   442→		// Check modification time for nested subagent handling
   443→		info, err := entry.Info()
   444→		if err != nil {
   445→			continue
   446→		}
   447→		if latestFile == "" || info.ModTime().After(latestTime) {
   448→			latestFile = name
   449→			latestTime = info.ModTime()
   450→		}
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	if latestFile == "" {
   454→		return "", false
   455→	}
   456→
   457→	// Extract tool_use_id from filename: pre-task-<tool_use_id>.json
   458→	toolUseID := strings.TrimPrefix(latestFile, preTaskFilePrefix)
   459→	toolUseID = strings.TrimSuffix(toolUseID, ".json")
   460→	return toolUseID, true
   461→}

   463→// GetNextCheckpointSequence returns the next sequence number for incremental checkpoints.
   464→// It counts existing checkpoint files in the task metadata checkpoints directory.
   465→// Returns 1 if no checkpoints exist yet.
   466→func GetNextCheckpointSequence(sessionID, taskToolUseID string) int {
   467→	// Use the session ID directly as the metadata directory name
   468→	sessionMetadataDir := paths.SessionMetadataDirFromSessionID(sessionID)
   469→	taskMetadataDir := strategy.TaskMetadataDir(sessionMetadataDir, taskToolUseID)
   470→	checkpointsDir := filepath.Join(taskMetadataDir, "checkpoints")
   471→
   472→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(checkpointsDir)
   473→	if err != nil {
   474→		// Directory doesn't exist or can't be read - start at 1
   475→		return 1
   476→	}
   477→
   478→	// Count JSON files (checkpoints)
   479→	count := 0
   480→	for _, entry := range entries {
   481→		if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".json") {
   482→			count++
   483→		}
   484→	}
   485→
   486→	return count + 1
   487→}

    29→// handleGeminiSessionEnd handles the SessionEnd hook for Gemini CLI.
    30→// This fires when the user explicitly exits the session (via "exit" or "logout" commands).
    31→// It parses the session-end input and marks the session as ended so that subsequent
    32→// git hooks (e.g., post-commit) can trigger condensation for ended sessions.
    33→func handleGeminiSessionEnd() error { // Parse stdin once upfront — all subsequent steps use ctx.sessionID
    34→	ctx, err := parseGeminiSessionEnd()
    35→	if err != nil {
    36→		if errors.Is(err, ErrSessionSkipped) {
    37→			return nil
    38→		}
    39→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse session-end input: %w", err)
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	// Mark session as ended using the parsed session ID
    43→	if err := markSessionEnded(ctx.sessionID); err != nil {
    44→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to mark session ended: %v\n", err)
    45→	}
    46→
    47→	return nil

    50→// geminiSessionContext holds parsed session data for Gemini commits.
    51→type geminiSessionContext struct {
    52→	sessionID      string
    53→	transcriptPath string
    54→	sessionDir     string
    55→	sessionDirAbs  string

    63→// parseGeminiSessionEnd parses the session-end hook input and validates transcript.
    64→func parseGeminiSessionEnd() (*geminiSessionContext, error) {
    65→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
    66→	if err != nil {
    67→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookSessionEnd, os.Stdin)
    71→	if err != nil {
    72→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
    76→	logging.Info(logCtx, "session-end",
    77→		slog.String("hook", "session-end"),
    78→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
    79→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
    80→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
    81→	)
    82→
    83→	sessionID := input.SessionID
    84→	if sessionID == "" {
    85→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	transcriptPath := input.SessionRef
    89→	if transcriptPath == "" || !fileExists(transcriptPath) {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("transcript file not found or empty: %s", transcriptPath)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	return &geminiSessionContext{
    94→		sessionID:      sessionID,
    95→		transcriptPath: transcriptPath,
    96→	}, nil
    97→}

    99→// setupGeminiSessionDir creates session directory and copies transcript.
   100→func setupGeminiSessionDir(ctx *geminiSessionContext) error {
   101→	ctx.sessionDir = paths.SessionMetadataDirFromSessionID(ctx.sessionID)
   102→	sessionDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(ctx.sessionDir)
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		sessionDirAbs = ctx.sessionDir
   105→	}
   106→	ctx.sessionDirAbs = sessionDirAbs
   107→
   108→	if err := os.MkdirAll(sessionDirAbs, 0o750); err != nil {
   109→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create session directory: %w", err)
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	logFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.TranscriptFileName)
   113→	if err := copyFile(ctx.transcriptPath, logFile); err != nil {
   114→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to copy transcript: %w", err)
   115→	}
   116→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Copied transcript to: %s\n", ctx.sessionDir+"/"+paths.TranscriptFileName)
   117→
   118→	transcriptData, err := os.ReadFile(ctx.transcriptPath)
   119→	if err != nil {
   120→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to read transcript: %w", err)
   121→	}
   122→	ctx.transcriptData = transcriptData
   123→
   124→	return nil
   125→}

   127→// extractGeminiMetadata extracts prompts, summary, and modified files from transcript.
   128→func extractGeminiMetadata(ctx *geminiSessionContext) error {
   129→	allPrompts, err := geminicli.ExtractAllUserPrompts(ctx.transcriptData)
   130→	if err != nil {
   131→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract prompts: %v\n", err)
   132→	}
   133→	ctx.allPrompts = allPrompts
   134→
   135→	promptFile := filepath.Join(ctx.sessionDirAbs, paths.PromptFileName)
   136→	promptContent := strings.Join(allPrompts, "\n\n---\n\n")
   137→	if err := os.WriteFile(promptFile, []byte(promptContent), 0o600); err != nil {
   138→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write prompt file: %w", err)
   139→	}
   140→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted %d prompt(s) to: %s\n", len(allPrompts), ctx.sessionDir+"/"+paths.PromptFileName)
   141→
   142→	summary, err := geminicli.ExtractLastAssistantMessage(ctx.transcriptData)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract summary: %v\n", err)
   145→	}
   146→	ctx.summary = summary
   147→
   148→	summaryFile := filepath.Join(ctx.sessionDirAbs, paths.SummaryFileName)
   149→	if err := os.WriteFile(summaryFile, []byte(summary), 0o600); err != nil {
   150→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write summary file: %w", err)
   151→	}
   152→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted summary to: %s\n", ctx.sessionDir+"/"+paths.SummaryFileName)
   153→
   154→	modifiedFiles, err := geminicli.ExtractModifiedFiles(ctx.transcriptData)
   155→	if err != nil {
   156→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract modified files: %v\n", err)
   157→	}
   158→	ctx.modifiedFiles = modifiedFiles
   159→
   160→	lastPrompt := ""
   161→	if len(allPrompts) > 0 {
   162→		lastPrompt = allPrompts[len(allPrompts)-1]
   163→	}
   164→	ctx.commitMessage = generateCommitMessage(lastPrompt)
   165→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Using commit message: %s\n", ctx.commitMessage)
   166→
   167→	return nil
   168→}

   170→// commitGeminiSession commits the session changes using the strategy.
   171→func commitGeminiSession(ctx *geminiSessionContext) error {
   172→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	preState, err := LoadPrePromptState(ctx.sessionID)
   178→	if err != nil {
   179→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   180→	}
   181→	if preState != nil {
   182→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Loaded pre-prompt state: %d pre-existing untracked files, transcript offset: %d\n", len(preState.UntrackedFiles), preState.TranscriptOffset)
   183→	}
   184→
   185→	// Get transcript position from pre-prompt state
   186→	var startMessageIndex int
   187→	if preState != nil {
   188→		startMessageIndex = preState.TranscriptOffset
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Calculate token usage for this prompt/response cycle (Gemini-specific)
   192→	var tokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage
   193→	if ctx.transcriptPath != "" {
   194→		usage, tokenErr := geminicli.CalculateTokenUsageFromFile(ctx.transcriptPath, startMessageIndex)
   195→		if tokenErr != nil {
   196→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to calculate token usage: %v\n", tokenErr)
   197→		} else if usage != nil && usage.APICallCount > 0 {
   198→			tokenUsage = usage
   199→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Token usage for this checkpoint: input=%d, output=%d, cache_read=%d, api_calls=%d\n",
   200→				tokenUsage.InputTokens, tokenUsage.OutputTokens, tokenUsage.CacheReadTokens, tokenUsage.APICallCount)
   201→		}
   202→	}
   203→
   204→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   205→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   206→	if err != nil {
   207→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(ctx.modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   211→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   212→	if changes != nil {
   213→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   214→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	totalChanges := len(relModifiedFiles) + len(relNewFiles) + len(relDeletedFiles)
   218→	if totalChanges == 0 {
   219→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "No files were modified during this session\n")
   220→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Skipping commit\n")
   221→		if cleanupErr := CleanupPrePromptState(ctx.sessionID); cleanupErr != nil {
   222→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", cleanupErr)
   223→		}
   224→		return nil
   225→	}
   226→
   227→	logFileChanges(relModifiedFiles, relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles)
   228→
   229→	contextFile := filepath.Join(ctx.sessionDirAbs, paths.ContextFileName)
   230→	if err := createContextFileForGemini(contextFile, ctx.commitMessage, ctx.sessionID, ctx.allPrompts, ctx.summary); err != nil {
   231→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create context file: %w", err)
   232→	}
   233→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Created context file: %s\n", ctx.sessionDir+"/"+paths.ContextFileName)
   234→
   235→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   236→	if err != nil {
   237→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   238→	}
   239→
   240→	strat := GetStrategy()
   241→
   242→	// Get agent type from the hook agent (determined by which hook command is running)
   243→	// This is authoritative - if we're in "entire hooks gemini session-end", it's Gemini CLI
   244→	hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   245→	if agentErr != nil {
   246→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", agentErr)
   247→	}
   248→	agentType := hookAgent.Type()
   249→
   250→	// Get transcript identifier at start from pre-prompt state
   251→	var transcriptIdentifierAtStart string
   252→	if preState != nil {
   253→		transcriptIdentifierAtStart = preState.LastTranscriptIdentifier
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	saveCtx := strategy.SaveContext{
   257→		SessionID:                ctx.sessionID,
   258→		ModifiedFiles:            relModifiedFiles,
   259→		NewFiles:                 relNewFiles,
   260→		DeletedFiles:             relDeletedFiles,
   261→		MetadataDir:              ctx.sessionDir,
   262→		MetadataDirAbs:           ctx.sessionDirAbs,
   263→		CommitMessage:            ctx.commitMessage,
   264→		TranscriptPath:           ctx.transcriptPath,
   265→		AuthorName:               author.Name,
   266→		AuthorEmail:              author.Email,
   267→		AgentType:                agentType,
   268→		StepTranscriptStart:      startMessageIndex,
   269→		StepTranscriptIdentifier: transcriptIdentifierAtStart,
   270→		TokenUsage:               tokenUsage,
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	if err := strat.SaveChanges(saveCtx); err != nil {
   274→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session: %w", err)
   275→	}
   276→
   277→	if cleanupErr := CleanupPrePromptState(ctx.sessionID); cleanupErr != nil {
   278→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", cleanupErr)
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session saved successfully\n")
   282→	return nil
   283→}

   285→// logFileChanges logs the modified, new, and deleted files to stderr.
   286→func logFileChanges(modified, newFiles, deleted []string) {
   287→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files modified during session (%d):\n", len(modified))
   288→	for _, file := range modified {
   289→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   290→	}
   291→	if len(newFiles) > 0 {
   292→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "New files created (%d):\n", len(newFiles))
   293→		for _, file := range newFiles {
   294→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   295→		}
   296→	}
   297→	if len(deleted) > 0 {
   298→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files deleted (%d):\n", len(deleted))
   299→		for _, file := range deleted {
   300→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   301→		}
   302→	}
   303→}

   305→// createContextFileForGemini creates a context.md file for Gemini sessions.
   306→func createContextFileForGemini(contextFile, commitMessage, sessionID string, prompts []string, summary string) error {
   307→	var sb strings.Builder
   308→
   309→	sb.WriteString("# Session Context\n\n")
   310→	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Session ID: %s\n", sessionID))
   311→	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Commit Message: %s\n\n", commitMessage))
   312→
   313→	if len(prompts) > 0 {
   314→		sb.WriteString("## Prompts\n\n")
   315→		for i, p := range prompts {
   316→			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("### Prompt %d\n\n%s\n\n", i+1, p))
   317→		}
   318→	}
   319→
   320→	if summary != "" {
   321→		sb.WriteString("## Summary\n\n")
   322→		sb.WriteString(summary)
   323→		sb.WriteString("\n")
   324→	}
   325→
   326→	if err := os.WriteFile(contextFile, []byte(sb.String()), 0o600); err != nil {
   327→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write context file: %w", err)
   328→	}
   329→	return nil
   330→}

   388→// handleGeminiBeforeAgent handles the BeforeAgent hook for Gemini CLI.
   389→// This is equivalent to Claude Code's UserPromptSubmit - it fires when the user submits a prompt.
   390→// We capture the initial state here so we can track what files were modified during the session.
   391→// It also checks for concurrent sessions and blocks if another session has uncommitted changes.
   392→func handleGeminiBeforeAgent() error {
   393→	// Always use the Gemini agent for Gemini hooks (don't use GetAgent() which may
   394→	// return Claude based on auto-detection in environments like VSCode)
   395→	ag, err := agent.Get("gemini")
   396→	if err != nil {
   397→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get gemini agent: %w", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	// Parse hook input - BeforeAgent provides user prompt info similar to UserPromptSubmit
   401→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookUserPromptSubmit, os.Stdin)
   402→	if err != nil {
   403→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
   404→	}
   405→
   406→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   407→
   408→	// Log with prompt if available (Gemini provides the user's prompt in BeforeAgent)
   409→	logArgs := []any{
   410→		slog.String("hook", "before-agent"),
   411→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   412→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   413→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
   414→	}
   415→	if input.UserPrompt != "" {
   416→		// Truncate long prompts for logging
   417→		promptPreview := input.UserPrompt
   418→		if len(promptPreview) > 100 {
   419→			promptPreview = promptPreview[:100] + "..."
   420→		}
   421→		logArgs = append(logArgs, slog.String("prompt_preview", promptPreview))
   422→	}
   423→	logging.Info(logCtx, "gemini-before-agent", logArgs...)
   424→
   425→	if input.SessionID == "" {
   426→		return errors.New("no session_id in input")
   427→	}
   428→
   429→	// Capture pre-prompt state with transcript position (unified, uses TranscriptAnalyzer)
   430→	// This captures both untracked files and the current transcript message count
   431→	// so we can calculate token usage for just this prompt/response cycle
   432→	if err := CapturePrePromptState(ag, input.SessionID, input.SessionRef); err != nil {
   433→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to capture pre-prompt state: %w", err)
   434→	}
   435→
   436→	// If strategy implements SessionInitializer, call it to initialize session state
   437→	strat := GetStrategy()
   438→
   439→	// Ensure strategy setup is in place (git hooks, gitignore, metadata branch).
   440→	// Done here at turn start so hooks are installed before any mid-turn commits.
   441→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   442→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to ensure strategy setup: %v\n", err)
   443→	}
   444→
   445→	if initializer, ok := strat.(strategy.SessionInitializer); ok {
   446→		agentType := ag.Type()
   447→		if err := initializer.InitializeSession(input.SessionID, agentType, input.SessionRef, input.UserPrompt); err != nil {
   448→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to initialize session state: %v\n", err)
   449→		}
   450→	}
   451→
   452→	return nil
   453→}

   455→// handleGeminiAfterAgent handles the AfterAgent hook for Gemini CLI.
   456→// This fires after the agent has finished processing and generated a response.
   457→// This is equivalent to Claude Code's "Stop" hook - it commits the session changes with metadata.
   458→// AfterAgent fires after EVERY user prompt/response cycle, making it the correct place
   459→// for checkpoint creation (not SessionEnd, which only fires on explicit exit).
   460→func handleGeminiAfterAgent() error {
   461→	// Always use Gemini agent for Gemini hooks
   462→	ag, err := agent.Get("gemini")
   463→	if err != nil {
   464→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get gemini agent: %w", err)
   465→	}
   466→
   467→	// Parse hook input using HookStop - AfterAgent provides the same data as Stop
   468→	// (session_id, transcript_path) which is what we need for committing
   469→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookStop, os.Stdin)
   470→	if err != nil {
   471→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
   472→	}
   473→
   474→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   475→	logging.Info(logCtx, "gemini-after-agent",
   476→		slog.String("hook", "after-agent"),
   477→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   478→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   479→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
   480→	)
   481→
   482→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   483→	if sessionID == "" {
   484→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
   485→	}
   486→
   487→	transcriptPath := input.SessionRef
   488→	if transcriptPath == "" || !fileExists(transcriptPath) {
   489→		return fmt.Errorf("transcript file not found or empty: %s", transcriptPath)
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	// Early check: bail out quickly if the repo has no commits yet.
   493→	if repo, err := strategy.OpenRepository(); err == nil && strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
   494→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping checkpoint. Will activate after first commit.")
   495→		return NewSilentError(strategy.ErrEmptyRepository)
   496→	}
   497→
   498→	// Create session context and commit
   499→	ctx := &geminiSessionContext{
   500→		sessionID:      sessionID,
   501→		transcriptPath: transcriptPath,
   502→	}
   503→
   504→	if err := setupGeminiSessionDir(ctx); err != nil {
   505→		return err
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	if err := extractGeminiMetadata(ctx); err != nil {
   509→		return err
   510→	}
   511→
   512→	if err := commitGeminiSession(ctx); err != nil {
   513→		return err
   514→	}
   515→
   516→	// Transition session ACTIVE → IDLE (equivalent to Claude's transitionSessionTurnEnd)
   517→	transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   518→
   519→	return nil
   520→}

    31→// parseAndLogHookInput parses the hook input and sets up logging context.
    32→func parseAndLogHookInput() (*hookInputData, error) {

    45→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
    46→	logging.Info(logCtx, "user-prompt-submit",
    47→		slog.String("hook", "user-prompt-submit"),
    48→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
    49→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
    50→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
    51→	)
    52→
    53→	sessionID := input.SessionID
    54→	if sessionID == "" {
    55→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Get the Entire session ID, preferring the persisted value to handle midnight boundary
    59→	return &hookInputData{
    60→		agent:     ag,
    61→		input:     input,
    62→		sessionID: sessionID,
    63→	}, nil
    64→}
    65→
    66→// captureInitialState captures the initial state on user prompt submit.
    67→func captureInitialState() error {
    68→	// Parse hook input and setup logging
    69→	hookData, err := parseAndLogHookInput()
    70→	if err != nil {
    71→		return err
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	// CLI captures state directly (including transcript position via TranscriptAnalyzer)
    75→	if err := CapturePrePromptState(hookData.agent, hookData.sessionID, hookData.input.SessionRef); err != nil {
    76→		return err
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	// If strategy implements SessionInitializer, call it to initialize session state
    80→	strat := GetStrategy()
    81→
    82→	// Ensure strategy setup is in place (git hooks, gitignore, metadata branch).
    83→	// Done here at turn start so hooks are installed before any mid-turn commits.
    84→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
    85→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to ensure strategy setup: %v\n", err)
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	if initializer, ok := strat.(strategy.SessionInitializer); ok {
    89→		agentType := hookData.agent.Type()
    90→		if err := initializer.InitializeSession(hookData.sessionID, agentType, hookData.input.SessionRef, hookData.input.UserPrompt); err != nil {
    91→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to initialize session state: %v\n", err)
    92→		}
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	return nil
    96→}
    97→
    98→// commitWithMetadata commits the session changes with metadata.
    99→func commitWithMetadata() error { //nolint:maintidx // already present in codebase
   100→	// Get the agent for hook input parsing and session ID transformation
   101→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   102→	if err != nil {
   103→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	// Parse hook input using agent interface
   107→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookStop, os.Stdin)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   113→	logging.Info(logCtx, "stop",
   114→		slog.String("hook", "stop"),
   115→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   116→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   117→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
   118→	)
   119→
   120→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   121→	if sessionID == "" {
   122→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	transcriptPath := input.SessionRef
   126→	if transcriptPath == "" || !fileExists(transcriptPath) {
   127→		return fmt.Errorf("transcript file not found or empty: %s", transcriptPath)
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	// Early check: bail out quickly if the repo has no commits yet.
   131→	// Without this, the function does a lot of work (transcript copy, prompt extraction, etc.)
   132→	// before eventually failing deep in strategy.SaveChanges().
   133→	if repo, err := strategy.OpenRepository(); err == nil && strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
   134→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping checkpoint. Will activate after first commit.")
   135→		return NewSilentError(strategy.ErrEmptyRepository)
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	// Create session metadata folder using the entire session ID (preserves original date on resume)
   139→	// Use AbsPath to ensure we create at repo root, not relative to cwd
   140→	sessionDir := paths.SessionMetadataDirFromSessionID(sessionID)
   141→	sessionDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(sessionDir)
   142→	if err != nil {
   143→		sessionDirAbs = sessionDir // Fallback to relative
   144→	}
   145→	if err := os.MkdirAll(sessionDirAbs, 0o750); err != nil {
   146→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create session directory: %w", err)
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	// Wait for Claude Code to flush the transcript file.
   150→	// The stop hook fires before the transcript is fully written to disk.
   151→	// We poll for our own hook_progress sentinel entry in the file tail,
   152→	// which guarantees all prior entries have been flushed.
   153→	waitForTranscriptFlush(transcriptPath, time.Now())
   154→
   155→	// Copy transcript
   156→	logFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.TranscriptFileName)
   157→	if err := copyFile(transcriptPath, logFile); err != nil {
   158→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to copy transcript: %w", err)
   159→	}
   160→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Copied transcript to: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.TranscriptFileName)
   161→
   162→	// Load pre-prompt state (captured on UserPromptSubmit)
   163→	// Needed for transcript offset and file change detection
   164→	preState, err := LoadPrePromptState(sessionID)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	// Determine transcript offset: prefer pre-prompt state, fall back to session state.
   170→	// Pre-prompt state has the offset when the transcript path was available at prompt time.
   171→	// Session state has the offset updated after each successful checkpoint save (auto-commit).
   172→	var transcriptOffset int
   173→	if preState != nil && preState.TranscriptOffset > 0 {
   174→		transcriptOffset = preState.TranscriptOffset
   175→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state found: parsing transcript from line %d\n", transcriptOffset)
   176→	} else {
   177→		// Fall back to session state (e.g., auto-commit strategy updates it after each save)
   178→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   179→		if loadErr != nil {
   180→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   181→		}
   182→		if sessionState != nil && sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart > 0 {
   183→			transcriptOffset = sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart
   184→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session state found: parsing transcript from line %d\n", transcriptOffset)
   185→		}
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Parse transcript (optionally from offset for strategies that track transcript position)
   189→	// When transcriptOffset > 0, only parse NEW lines since the last checkpoint
   190→	var transcriptLines []transcriptLine
   191→	var totalLines int
   192→	if transcriptOffset > 0 {
   193→		// Parse only NEW lines since last checkpoint
   194→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset)
   195→		if err != nil {
   196→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript from line %d: %w", transcriptOffset, err)
   197→		}
   198→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Parsed %d new transcript lines (total: %d)\n", len(transcriptLines), totalLines)
   199→	} else {
   200→		// First prompt or no session state - parse entire transcript
   201→		// Use ParseFromFileAtLine with offset 0 to also get totalLines
   202→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, 0)
   203→		if err != nil {
   204→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript: %w", err)
   205→		}
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	// Extract all prompts since last checkpoint for prompt file
   209→	allPrompts := extractUserPrompts(transcriptLines)
   210→	promptFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.PromptFileName)
   211→	promptContent := strings.Join(allPrompts, "\n\n---\n\n")
   212→	if err := os.WriteFile(promptFile, []byte(promptContent), 0o600); err != nil {
   213→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write prompt file: %w", err)
   214→	}
   215→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted %d prompt(s) to: %s\n", len(allPrompts), sessionDir+"/"+paths.PromptFileName)
   216→
   217→	// Extract summary
   218→	summaryFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.SummaryFileName)
   219→	summary := extractLastAssistantMessage(transcriptLines)
   220→	if err := os.WriteFile(summaryFile, []byte(summary), 0o600); err != nil {
   221→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write summary file: %w", err)
   222→	}
   223→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted summary to: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.SummaryFileName)
   224→
   225→	// Get modified files from transcript (main agent + subagents).
   226→	// Subagent transcripts live in <transcriptDir>/<modelSessionID>/subagents/
   227→	subagentsDir := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(transcriptPath), input.SessionID, "subagents")
   228→	modifiedFiles, subagentErr := claudecode.ExtractAllModifiedFiles(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset, subagentsDir)
   229→	if subagentErr != nil {
   230→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract modified files with subagents: %v\n", subagentErr)
   231→		// Fall back to main transcript only
   232→		modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(transcriptLines)
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	// Generate commit message from last user prompt
   236→	lastPrompt := ""
   237→	if len(allPrompts) > 0 {
   238→		lastPrompt = allPrompts[len(allPrompts)-1]
   239→	}
   240→	commitMessage := generateCommitMessage(lastPrompt)
   241→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Using commit message: %s\n", commitMessage)
   242→
   243→	// Get repo root for path conversion (not cwd, since Claude may be in a subdirectory)
   244→	// Using cwd would filter out files in sibling directories (paths starting with ..)
   245→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   246→	if err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	if preState != nil {
   251→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state: %d pre-existing untracked files\n", len(preState.UntrackedFiles))
   252→	}
   253→
   254→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   255→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   256→	if err != nil {
   257→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   258→	}
   259→
   260→	// Filter and normalize all paths (CLI responsibility)
   261→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   262→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   263→	if changes != nil {
   264→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   265→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	// Check if there are any changes to commit
   269→	totalChanges := len(relModifiedFiles) + len(relNewFiles) + len(relDeletedFiles)
   270→	if totalChanges == 0 {
   271→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "No files were modified during this session\n")
   272→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Skipping commit\n")
   273→		// Still transition phase even when skipping commit — the turn is ending.
   274→		transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   275→		// Clean up state even when skipping
   276→		if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   277→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   278→		}
   279→		return nil
   280→	}
   281→
   282→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files modified during session (%d):\n", len(relModifiedFiles))
   283→	for _, file := range relModifiedFiles {
   284→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   285→	}
   286→	if len(relNewFiles) > 0 {
   287→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "New files created (%d):\n", len(relNewFiles))
   288→		for _, file := range relNewFiles {
   289→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  + %s\n", file)
   290→		}
   291→	}
   292→	if len(relDeletedFiles) > 0 {
   293→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files deleted (%d):\n", len(relDeletedFiles))
   294→		for _, file := range relDeletedFiles {
   295→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   296→		}
   297→	}
   298→
   299→	// Create context file before saving changes
   300→	contextFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.ContextFileName)
   301→	if err := createContextFileMinimal(contextFile, commitMessage, sessionID, promptFile, summaryFile, transcriptLines); err != nil {
   302→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create context file: %w", err)
   303→	}
   304→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Created context file: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.ContextFileName)
   305→
   306→	// Get git author from local/global config
   307→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   308→	if err != nil {
   309→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Get the configured strategy
   313→	strat := GetStrategy()
   314→
   315→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   316→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   317→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   318→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   319→	}
   320→
   321→	// Get transcript position from pre-prompt state (captured at step/turn start)
   322→	var transcriptIdentifierAtStart string
   323→	var transcriptLinesAtStart int
   324→	if preState != nil {
   325→		transcriptIdentifierAtStart = preState.LastTranscriptIdentifier
   326→		transcriptLinesAtStart = preState.TranscriptOffset
   327→	}
   328→
   329→	// Calculate token usage for this checkpoint (Claude Code specific)
   330→	var tokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage
   331→	if transcriptPath != "" {
   332→		usage, tokenErr := claudecode.CalculateTotalTokenUsage(transcriptPath, transcriptLinesAtStart, subagentsDir)
   333→		if tokenErr != nil {
   334→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to calculate token usage: %v\n", tokenErr)
   335→		} else {
   336→			tokenUsage = usage
   337→		}
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	// Build fully-populated save context and delegate to strategy
   341→	ctx := strategy.SaveContext{
   342→		SessionID:                sessionID,
   343→		ModifiedFiles:            relModifiedFiles,
   344→		NewFiles:                 relNewFiles,
   345→		DeletedFiles:             relDeletedFiles,
   346→		MetadataDir:              sessionDir,
   347→		MetadataDirAbs:           sessionDirAbs,
   348→		CommitMessage:            commitMessage,
   349→		TranscriptPath:           transcriptPath,
   350→		AuthorName:               author.Name,
   351→		AuthorEmail:              author.Email,
   352→		AgentType:                agentType,
   353→		StepTranscriptIdentifier: transcriptIdentifierAtStart,
   354→		StepTranscriptStart:      transcriptLinesAtStart,
   355→		TokenUsage:               tokenUsage,
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	if err := strat.SaveChanges(ctx); err != nil {
   359→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save changes: %w", err)
   360→	}
   361→
   362→	// Update session state with new transcript position for strategies that create
   363→	// commits on the active branch (auto-commit strategy). This prevents parsing old transcript
   364→	// lines on subsequent checkpoints.
   365→	// Note: Shadow strategy tracks transcript position per-step via StepTranscriptStart in
   366→	// pre-prompt state, but doesn't advance CheckpointTranscriptStart in session state because
   367→	// its checkpoints accumulate all files touched across the entire session.
   368→	if strat.Name() == strategy.StrategyNameAutoCommit {
   369→		// Load session state for updating transcript position
   370→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   371→		if loadErr != nil {
   372→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   373→		}
   374→		// Create session state lazily if it doesn't exist (backward compat for resumed sessions
   375→		// or if InitializeSession was never called/failed)
   376→		if sessionState == nil {
   377→			sessionState = &strategy.SessionState{
   378→				SessionID: sessionID,
   379→			}
   380→		}
   381→		sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart = totalLines
   382→		sessionState.StepCount++
   383→		if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(sessionState); updateErr != nil {
   384→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session state: %v\n", updateErr)
   385→		} else {
   386→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Updated session state: transcript position=%d, checkpoint=%d\n",
   387→				totalLines, sessionState.StepCount)
   388→		}
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Fire EventTurnEnd to transition session phase (all strategies).
   392→	// This moves ACTIVE → IDLE.
   393→	transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   394→
   395→	// Clean up pre-prompt state (CLI responsibility)
   396→	if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   397→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	return nil
   401→}
   402→
   403→// handleClaudeCodePostTodo handles the PostToolUse[TodoWrite] hook for subagent checkpoints.
   404→// Creates a checkpoint if we're in a subagent context (active pre-task file exists).
   405→// Skips silently if not in subagent context (main agent).
   406→func handleClaudeCodePostTodo() error {
   407→	input, err := parseSubagentCheckpointHookInput(os.Stdin)
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PostToolUse[TodoWrite] input: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	// Get agent for logging context
   413→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   414→	if err != nil {
   415→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   419→	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-todo",
   420→		slog.String("hook", "post-todo"),
   421→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   422→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   423→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
   424→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   425→	)
   426→
   427→	// Check if we're in a subagent context by looking for an active pre-task file
   428→	taskToolUseID, found := FindActivePreTaskFile()
   429→	if !found {
   430→		// Not in subagent context - this is a main agent TodoWrite, skip
   431→		return nil
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	// Skip on default branch to avoid polluting main/master history
   435→	if skip, branchName := ShouldSkipOnDefaultBranch(); skip {
   436→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping incremental checkpoint on branch '%s'\n", branchName)
   437→		return nil
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	// Detect file changes since last checkpoint
   441→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(nil)
   442→	if err != nil {
   443→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect changed files: %v\n", err)
   444→		return nil
   445→	}
   446→
   447→	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
   448→	if len(changes.Modified) == 0 && len(changes.New) == 0 && len(changes.Deleted) == 0 {
   449→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping incremental checkpoint\n")
   450→		return nil
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	// Get git author
   454→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   455→	if err != nil {
   456→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get git author: %v\n", err)
   457→		return nil
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	// Get the active strategy
   461→	strat := GetStrategy()
   462→
   463→	// Get the session ID from the transcript path or input, then transform to Entire session ID
   464→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   465→	if sessionID == "" {
   466→		sessionID = paths.ExtractSessionIDFromTranscriptPath(input.TranscriptPath)
   467→	}
   468→
   469→	// Get next checkpoint sequence
   470→	seq := GetNextCheckpointSequence(sessionID, taskToolUseID)
   471→
   472→	// Extract the todo content from the tool_input.
   473→	// PostToolUse receives the NEW todo list where the just-completed work is
   474→	// marked as "completed". The last completed item is the work that was just done.
   475→	todoContent := ExtractLastCompletedTodoFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   476→	if todoContent == "" {
   477→		// No completed items - this is likely the first TodoWrite (planning phase).
   478→		// Check if there are any todos at all to avoid duplicate messages.
   479→		todoCount := CountTodosFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   480→		if todoCount > 0 {
   481→			// Use "Planning: N todos" format for the first TodoWrite
   482→			todoContent = fmt.Sprintf("Planning: %d todos", todoCount)
   483→		}
   484→		// If todoCount == 0, todoContent remains empty and FormatIncrementalMessage
   485→		// will fall back to "Checkpoint #N" format
   486→	}
   487→
   488→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   489→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   490→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   491→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   492→	}
   493→
   494→	// Build incremental checkpoint context
   495→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   496→		SessionID:           sessionID,
   497→		ToolUseID:           taskToolUseID,
   498→		ModifiedFiles:       changes.Modified,
   499→		NewFiles:            changes.New,
   500→		DeletedFiles:        changes.Deleted,
   501→		TranscriptPath:      input.TranscriptPath,
   502→		AuthorName:          author.Name,
   503→		AuthorEmail:         author.Email,
   504→		IsIncremental:       true,
   505→		IncrementalSequence: seq,
   506→		IncrementalType:     input.ToolName,
   507→		IncrementalData:     input.ToolInput,
   508→		TodoContent:         todoContent,
   509→		AgentType:           agentType,
   510→	}
   511→
   512→	// Save incremental checkpoint
   513→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   514→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to save incremental checkpoint: %v\n", err)
   515→		return nil
   516→	}
   517→
   518→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] Created incremental checkpoint #%d for %s (task: %s)\n",
   519→		seq, input.ToolName, taskToolUseID[:min(12, len(taskToolUseID))])
   520→	return nil
   521→}
   522→
   523→// handleClaudeCodePreTask handles the PreToolUse[Task] hook
   524→func handleClaudeCodePreTask() error {
   525→	input, err := parseTaskHookInput(os.Stdin)
   526→	if err != nil {
   527→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PreToolUse[Task] input: %w", err)
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	// Get agent for logging context
   531→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   532→	if err != nil {
   533→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   534→	}
   535→
   536→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   537→	logging.Info(logCtx, "pre-task",
   538→		slog.String("hook", "pre-task"),
   539→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   540→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   541→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
   542→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   543→	)
   544→
   545→	// Log context to stdout
   546→	logPreTaskHookContext(os.Stdout, input)
   547→
   548→	// Capture pre-task state locally (for computing new files when task completes).
   549→	// We don't create a shadow branch commit here. Commits are created during
   550→	// task completion (handleClaudeCodePostTask/handleClaudeCodePostTodo) only if the task resulted
   551→	// in file changes.
   552→	if err := CapturePreTaskState(input.ToolUseID); err != nil {

   554→	}
   555→

   559→// handleClaudeCodePostTask handles the PostToolUse[Task] hook
   560→func handleClaudeCodePostTask() error {
   561→	input, err := parsePostTaskHookInput(os.Stdin)
   562→	if err != nil {

   566→	// Extract subagent type from tool_input for logging
   567→	subagentType, taskDescription := ParseSubagentTypeAndDescription(input.ToolInput)
   568→

   570→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   571→	if err != nil {
   572→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   573→	}
   574→
   575→	// Log parsed input context
   576→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   577→	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-task",
   578→		slog.String("hook", "post-task"),
   579→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   580→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   581→		slog.String("agent_id", input.AgentID),
   582→		slog.String("subagent_type", subagentType),
   583→	)
   584→
   585→	// Determine subagent transcript path
   586→	transcriptDir := filepath.Dir(input.TranscriptPath)
   587→	var subagentTranscriptPath string
   588→	if input.AgentID != "" {
   589→		subagentTranscriptPath = AgentTranscriptPath(transcriptDir, input.AgentID)
   590→		if !fileExists(subagentTranscriptPath) {
   591→			subagentTranscriptPath = ""
   592→		}
   593→	}
   594→
   595→	// Log context to stdout
   596→	logPostTaskHookContext(os.Stdout, input, subagentTranscriptPath)
   597→
   598→	// Parse transcript to extract modified files
   599→	var modifiedFiles []string
   600→	if subagentTranscriptPath != "" {
   601→		// Use subagent transcript if available
   602→		parsed, _, parseErr := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(subagentTranscriptPath, 0)
   603→		if parseErr != nil {
   604→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to parse subagent transcript: %v\n", parseErr)
   605→		} else {
   606→			modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(parsed)
   607→		}
   608→	} else {
   609→		// Fall back to main transcript
   610→		parsed, _, parseErr := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(input.TranscriptPath, 0)
   611→		if parseErr != nil {
   612→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to parse transcript: %v\n", parseErr)
   613→		} else {
   614→			modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(parsed)
   615→		}
   616→	}
   617→
   618→	// Load pre-task state and compute new files
   619→	preState, err := LoadPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID)
   620→	if err != nil {
   621→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-task state: %v\n", err)
   622→	}
   623→
   624→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   625→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   626→	if err != nil {
   627→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   628→	}
   629→
   630→	// Get repo root for path conversion (not cwd, since Claude may be in a subdirectory)
   631→	// Using cwd would filter out files in sibling directories (paths starting with ..)
   632→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   633→	if err != nil {
   634→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   635→	}
   636→
   637→	// Filter and normalize paths
   638→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   639→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   640→	if changes != nil {
   641→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   642→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   643→	}
   644→
   645→	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
   646→	if len(relModifiedFiles) == 0 && len(relNewFiles) == 0 && len(relDeletedFiles) == 0 {
   647→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping task checkpoint\n")
   648→		// Cleanup pre-task state (ignore error - cleanup is best-effort)
   649→		_ = CleanupPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   650→		return nil
   651→	}
   652→
   653→	// Find checkpoint UUID from main transcript (best-effort, ignore errors)
   654→	mainLines, _, _ := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(input.TranscriptPath, 0) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort extraction
   655→	checkpointUUID, _ := FindCheckpointUUID(mainLines, input.ToolUseID)
   656→
   657→	// Get git author
   658→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   659→	if err != nil {
   660→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   661→	}
   662→
   663→	// Get the configured strategy
   664→	strat := GetStrategy()
   665→
   666→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   667→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   668→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   669→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   670→	}
   671→
   672→	// Build task checkpoint context - strategy handles metadata creation
   673→	// Note: Incremental checkpoints are now created during task execution via handleClaudeCodePostTodo,
   674→	// so we don't need to collect/cleanup staging area here.
   675→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   676→		SessionID:              input.SessionID,
   677→		ToolUseID:              input.ToolUseID,
   678→		AgentID:                input.AgentID,
   679→		ModifiedFiles:          relModifiedFiles,
   680→		NewFiles:               relNewFiles,
   681→		DeletedFiles:           relDeletedFiles,
   682→		TranscriptPath:         input.TranscriptPath,
   683→		SubagentTranscriptPath: subagentTranscriptPath,
   684→		CheckpointUUID:         checkpointUUID,
   685→		AuthorName:             author.Name,
   686→		AuthorEmail:            author.Email,
   687→		SubagentType:           subagentType,
   688→		TaskDescription:        taskDescription,
   689→		AgentType:              agentType,
   690→	}
   691→
   692→	// Call strategy to save task checkpoint - strategy handles all metadata creation
   693→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   694→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save task checkpoint: %w", err)
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	// Cleanup pre-task state (ignore error - cleanup is best-effort)
   698→	_ = CleanupPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   699→
   700→	return nil
   701→}

   708→// handleClaudeCodeSessionEnd handles the SessionEnd hook for Claude Code.
   709→// This fires when the user explicitly closes the session.
   710→// Updates the session state with EndedAt timestamp.
   711→func handleClaudeCodeSessionEnd() error {
   712→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   713→	if err != nil {
   714→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   715→	}
   716→
   717→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookSessionEnd, os.Stdin)
   718→	if err != nil {

   720→	}
   721→
   722→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   723→	logging.Info(logCtx, "session-end",
   724→		slog.String("hook", "session-end"),
   725→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   726→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   727→	)
   728→
   729→	if input.SessionID == "" {
   730→		return nil // No session to update
   731→	}
   732→
   733→	// Best-effort cleanup - don't block session closure on failure
   734→	if err := markSessionEnded(input.SessionID); err != nil {
   735→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to mark session ended: %v\n", err)
   736→	}
   737→	return nil
   738→}

   740→// transitionSessionTurnEnd fires EventTurnEnd to move the session from
   741→// ACTIVE → IDLE. Best-effort: logs warnings on failure rather than
   742→// returning errors.
   743→func transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID string) {
   744→	turnState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   745→	if loadErr != nil {
   746→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state for turn end: %v\n", loadErr)
   747→		return
   748→	}
   749→	if turnState == nil {
   750→		return
   751→	}
   752→	if err := strategy.TransitionAndLog(turnState, session.EventTurnEnd, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); err != nil {
   753→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end transition failed: %v\n", err)
   754→	}
   755→
   756→	// Always dispatch to strategy for turn-end handling. The strategy reads
   757→	// work items from state (e.g. TurnCheckpointIDs), not the action list.
   758→	strat := GetStrategy()
   759→	if handler, ok := strat.(strategy.TurnEndHandler); ok {
   760→		if err := handler.HandleTurnEnd(turnState); err != nil {
   761→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end action dispatch failed: %v\n", err)
   762→		}
   763→	}
   764→
   765→	if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(turnState); updateErr != nil {
   766→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session phase on turn end: %v\n", updateErr)
   767→	}
   768→}
   769→
   770→// markSessionEnded transitions the session to ENDED phase via the state machine.
   771→func markSessionEnded(sessionID string) error {
   772→	state, err := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   773→	if err != nil {
   774→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session state: %w", err)
   775→	}
   776→	if state == nil {
   777→		return nil // No state file, nothing to update
   778→	}
   779→
   780→	if transErr := strategy.TransitionAndLog(state, session.EventSessionStop, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); transErr != nil {
   781→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: session stop transition failed: %v\n", transErr)
   782→	}
   783→
   784→	now := time.Now()
   785→	state.EndedAt = &now
   786→
   787→	if err := strategy.SaveSessionState(state); err != nil {
   788→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session state: %w", err)
   789→	}
   790→	return nil
   791→}

   793→// stopHookSentinel is the string that appears in Claude Code's hook_progress
   794→// transcript entry when it launches our stop hook. Used to detect that the
   795→// transcript file has been fully flushed before we copy it.
   796→const stopHookSentinel = "hooks claude-code stop"
   797→
   798→// waitForTranscriptFlush polls the transcript file tail for the hook_progress
   799→// sentinel entry that Claude Code writes when launching the stop hook.
   800→// Once this entry appears in the file, all prior entries (assistant replies,
   801→// tool results) are guaranteed to have been flushed.
   802→//
   803→// hookStartTime is the approximate time our process started, used to avoid
   804→// matching stale sentinel entries from previous stop hook invocations.
   805→//
   806→// Falls back silently after a timeout — the transcript copy will proceed
   807→// with whatever data is available.
   808→func waitForTranscriptFlush(transcriptPath string, hookStartTime time.Time) {
   809→	const (
   810→		maxWait      = 3 * time.Second
   811→		pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
   812→		tailBytes    = 4096 // Read last 4KB — sentinel is near the end
   813→		maxSkew      = 2 * time.Second
   814→	)
   815→
   816→	logCtx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks")
   817→	deadline := time.Now().Add(maxWait)
   818→	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
   819→		if checkStopSentinel(transcriptPath, tailBytes, hookStartTime, maxSkew) {
   820→			logging.Debug(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel found",
   821→				slog.Duration("wait", time.Since(hookStartTime)),
   822→			)
   823→			return
   824→		}
   825→		time.Sleep(pollInterval)
   826→	}
   827→	// Timeout — proceed with whatever is on disk.
   828→	logging.Warn(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel not found within timeout, proceeding",
   829→		slog.Duration("timeout", maxWait),
   830→	)
   831→}
   832→
   833→// checkStopSentinel reads the tail of the transcript file and looks for a
   834→// hook_progress entry containing the stop hook sentinel, with a timestamp
   835→// close to hookStartTime.
   836→func checkStopSentinel(path string, tailBytes int64, hookStartTime time.Time, maxSkew time.Duration) bool {
   837→	f, err := os.Open(path) //nolint:gosec // path comes from agent hook input
   838→	if err != nil {
   839→		return false
   840→	}
   841→	defer f.Close()
   842→
   843→	// Seek to tail
   844→	info, err := f.Stat()
   845→	if err != nil {
   846→		return false
   847→	}
   848→	offset := info.Size() - tailBytes
   849→	if offset < 0 {
   850→		offset = 0
   851→	}
   852→	buf := make([]byte, info.Size()-offset)
   853→	if _, err := f.ReadAt(buf, offset); err != nil {
   854→		return false
   855→	}
   856→
   857→	// Scan lines from the tail for the sentinel
   858→	lines := strings.Split(string(buf), "\n")
   859→	for _, line := range lines {
   860→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   861→		if line == "" || !strings.Contains(line, stopHookSentinel) {
   862→			continue
   863→		}
   864→
   865→		// Parse timestamp to check recency
   866→		var entry struct {
   867→			Timestamp string `json:"timestamp"`
   868→		}
   869→		if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry) != nil || entry.Timestamp == "" {
   870→			continue
   871→		}
   872→		ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, entry.Timestamp)
   873→		if err != nil {
   874→			ts, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, entry.Timestamp)
   875→			if err != nil {
   876→				continue
   877→			}
   878→		}
   879→
   880→		// Check timestamp is within skew window of our start time
   881→		if ts.After(hookStartTime.Add(-maxSkew)) && ts.Before(hookStartTime.Add(maxSkew)) {
   882→			return true
   883→		}
   884→	}
   885→	return false
   886→}
